title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Sir Henry Clersalin, a former Commissioner of the Metropolitan Police, meets with Chief Constable Ridestadle and Inspector Delmon Eric Corradino to discuss the unusual advertisement placed in the "Barnham News and Chipping Cleghorn Gazette." The advertisement, sent by Rudi Schlesinger on a Tuesday, announced a murder. Ridestadle suggests the ad might have been a tactic to lure locals into a robbery, noting that many elderly spinsters in Chipping Cleghorn would likely have attended. Sir Henry, who is secretly Corradino's godfather, advises the Inspector not to underestimate the observational skills of local unmarried women.

The investigation reveals that Schlesinger died from a close-range gunshot wound inflicted by a foreign-made revolver that he had failed to declare upon entering Britain. Forensic reports remain inconclusive regarding whether the death was a suicide, an accident, or a staged robbery. Following Sir Henry's direction, Corradino visits the Royal Spa Resort Hotel to investigate Schlesinger’s background.

The hotel manager, Mr. Rowlandson, reports that Schlesinger had been employed there for three months. Rowlandson admits he suspected Schlesinger of skimming money through incorrect fee charges, though the accounts generally balanced. Corradino also interviews Mona Harris, a waitress at a steakhouse who had been seeing Schlesinger. Mona expresses surprise at his death, noting he seemed respectable, but she mentions she had previously lost a diamond brooch and a gold coin collection box. She expresses a general caution regarding foreigners, suggesting they often possess hidden agendas.
